name: Gateau Rouge Diable (Red Devil Cake)
recipeIngredient: 4 tsp artificial sweetener (sugar twin is one that remains sweet after cooking)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put raisins in a small bowl and pour over 1/2 cup boiling water. Set aside to steep for 20 minutes.
2. In a large bowl, blend together cocoa and 3/4 cup boiling water. Set aside to cool.
3. Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly coat a 9-inch cake pan with non-stick spray. Dust pan with 1 or 2 tablespoons flour, coating completely.
4. Over a medium bowl, sift together flour, baking soda, and salt.
5. Add raisins and water to the beaker of a hand-held mixer or blender and puree.
6. Add beets and 3/4 of the cool water to the puree. Dissolve cornstarch in remaining 1/4 cup water.
7. Stir raisin and beet puree, dissolved cornstarch, sugar, and vanilla into cocoa mixture.
8. Quickly stir dry ingredients into cocoa mixture. Do not stir longer than needed to thoroughly mix the two.
9. Scrape batter into prepared pan and smooth the surface.
10. Bake on top shelf for 35 to 40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the middle of the cake comes out clean.
11. Let cake sit in pan for 10 to 15 minutes, then remove to a wire rack. Cool cake completely before cutting.
